title: Understanding Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Rotational kinetic energy is given by the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \), where \( I \) is the moment of inertia and \( \omega \) is the angular velocity.

title: Relating Linear and Angular Velocity
text: The linear velocity \( v \) is related to the angular velocity \( \omega \) by the equation \( v $$=$$ $$r$$ \omega \), where \( $$r$$ \) is the radius or distance from the axis of rotation.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Using the relation \( v $$=$$ $$r$$ \omega \), calculate \( \omega \) given \( v $$=$$ $$20.0$$ \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and \( $$r$$ $$=$$ $$0.480$$ \) $$m$$.

title: Calculate Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Now that you have \( \omega \), use the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \) to find the rotational kinetic energy. Use \( I $$=$$ $$0.500$$ \) $$kg m^2$$ and \( \omega $$=$$ $$41.67$$ \) $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.
